title: Quamin

'''Quamin''' (plural '''quamilla''') were flying predators from the [[Kidron/Legends|Kidron]] jungles. They had leathery skin, membranous wings, and a long tail. Their tails had razor-sharp fins, which quamilla used as their primary weapons. Their heads did not contain [[Mouth/Legends|mouths]], instead, quamilla sucked [[Blood/Legends|blood]] and other vital fluids from their prey through an array of spongy feeding vents on their head.<ref name="Creatures of the Galaxy" />

Internally, quamilla had three [[Heart/Legends|hearts]], large [[Lung/Legends|lungs]] which compensated for Kidron's thin [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], hollow [[Bone/Legends|bones]], and surprisingly powerful muscles. Redundancy in their internal organs gave them superior endurance, and allowed them to heal quickly from internal injuries. They also had a keen sense of smell, which they used instead of vision as their primary sense. Their tail blades were their main weakness: if a quamin's tail blade broke, it would take a standard week to grow back, during which it would be unable to hunt, and the injured quamin could starve.<ref name="Creatures of the Galaxy" />

Quamilla usually traveled in packs, or flights, of five to ten members. Despite cooperating to kill large prey, quamilla did not seem to get along well. Once they had made a kill, they would frequently fight over the best portions. They would also fight for dominance in the pack. not territorial.<ref name="Creatures of the Galaxy" />

Quamilla would fly silently through the skies, swooping down to attack larger creatures or groups of creatures in waves. They did not kill their prey instantly, rather, they lashed them with their tail fins, then followed their quarry until they collapsed from blood loss. The quamilla then sucked the fluids from their prey, leaving the husks for [[slork]]s.<ref name="Creatures of the Galaxy" />

Travelers to Kidron were often advised to be wary when they traveled through Kidron's forests and hills, lest they fell prey to a quamin attack. However, the people of Kidron did find at least one use for quamilla. Quamin tail blades were used as weapons by some [[Orfite]]s on Kidron, as well as by certain offworlders. They could be tied to poles, woven into whips, or used as throwing weapons. It was said that such blades never dulled, though they could break fairly easily.<ref name="Creatures of the Galaxy" />

A quamilla population was also found on [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]]. In arranging his elaborate trap on Nal Hutta for [[Jodo Kast]], [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] had [[Dengar/Legends|Dengar]] round up a number of quamilla which were released to attack Kast upon his arrival. Dengar described the operation as being more of a challenge than some of his hunts, and the quamilla as "lovable leeches".<ref name="Boba Fett: Twin Engines of Destruction" />
